Remarks:
There were two birds. Our guide, upon hearing them, used a recording of their call in an attempt to bring them out of the deep foliage. One bird flew across the trail and was gone; I did not see it, but the guide did. The other bird continued to call; this recording is that 2nd bird (this one was never seen). 

The next day we finally had good looks at several of these birds near the same area, but they were silent.

An agitated Philippine Tailorbird https://www.xeno-canto.org/species/Orthotomus-castaneiceps

Original request;
Second recording: This Bird is a daily caller here, it has a repetitive call similar to but not the same as so many recordings online. This Bird is repetitive, every day it has the same selected set of calls in groups of 3 or 4 before it changes the tune of the calls, again repetitive. I have been led to listen to recordings which could possibly be this Bird but at the end of the day have dismissed them all as none have exactly the same calls which I hear here on a daily basis. It is driving me crazy trying to work out what this Bird is as it always remains well hidden in the Bamboo or Mango Trees when calling. I hope someone can identify it.
